verdict_reasoning: SRC (c-Src) is an N-myristoylated non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via its myristoyl group and polybasic SH4 cluster — not the extracellular face. However, a 2026 Science study provides gene-specific experimental evidence that in cancer cells, Src is topologically inverted onto the extracellular surface through autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), generating extracellular membrane-associated Src (eSrc) detectable in primary tumors. Anti-Src antibody-based therapies exploiting this eSrc pool showed tumor cell killing in vitro and in xenograft models. This outer-leaflet display is cancer-state-specific (non-baseline), qualifying as cell_state_induced. At baseline in normal cells, Src body is strictly inner-leaflet-anchored and inaccessible from outside. The five contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced: YES — cancer-specific ALE-mediated outer-leaflet inversion documented gene-specifically; (2) tissue_restricted_surface: no evidence of germline/developmental display; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis: the ALE mechanism is functionally related but this is the cell_state_induced cancer mechanism; (4) dual_localization: no constitutive PM-outer-leaflet pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment: not applicable.
